vocab.meaning: một thành công rực rỡ/ấn tượng
vocab.example: The decision to host the regional tournament was a home run for our city's tourism and local pride.

vocab.meaning: gỡ hòa (nghĩa bóng: trả đũa hoặc làm cho tình thế cân bằng)
vocab.example: Team sports are popular because they give people a chance to level the score against historical rivals on the pitch.

vocab.meaning: nhạy bén, hiểu nhanh vấn đề
vocab.example: In our culture, coaches expect young players to be on the ball both in terms of tactics and sportsmanship.

vocab.meaning: người dẫn đầu, ứng cử viên sáng giá nhất
vocab.example: Football is undoubtedly the front-runner when it comes to the most watched and played sports in my country.

vocab.meaning: mắc sai lầm do bất cẩn
vocab.example: When the national team drops the ball in a major game, you can feel a collective sense of disappointment across the nation.

vocab.meaning: một cục diện hoàn toàn mới/tình huống khác biệt
vocab.example: Watching a match in the stadium is a whole new ball game compared to just viewing it on a television screen.

vocab.meaning: người đưa ra quyết định, chỉ huy
vocab.example: While fans love to debate, it's ultimately the head coach who calls the shots and determines the team's destiny.

vocab.meaning: người/tổ chức có tầm ảnh hưởng lớn, quyền lực
vocab.example: Our local league has several heavy hitters—teams with massive budgets and star-studded lineups.

vocab.meaning: chuyện thường tình, không có gì đáng ngạc nhiên
vocab.example: Intense celebrations after a victory are par for the course in a country so obsessed with collective sports.
Even when facing much stronger opponents, our national teams are taught never to throw in the towel until the final whistle.
